expectations.text: Identifies the PDF as Type A (Word-generated) based on the Unicode math copy-paste clue
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly identifies the Unicode math symbols (∑, π, σ) as 'the definitive sign of a Type A Word-generated PDF' and leads with this diagnosis.

expectations.text: Recommends pymupdf4llm as the prose extraction tool
expectations.evidence: Response recommends pymupdf4llm with the uv run command.

expectations.text: Explicitly warns that marker-pdf will NOT work on this PDF type
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states 'Never use marker-pdf for this PDF type' and explains it fails silently.

expectations.text: States that math equations must be manually transcribed from PDF screenshots
expectations.evidence: Response states 'there is no shortcut' and provides 3-step manual transcription process.

expectations.text: Mentions the $$ vs ```math distinction or multi-line rendering rule
expectations.evidence: Response covers the $$-vs-```math decision including the pre-processor stripping issue.
